data.title: Wintermute founder: BTC and ETH will continue to rise, but there may not be a full bull market. The market will focus on protocols with sustainable economic models
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Wintermute founder and CEO Evgeny Gaevoy said today in Consensus Hong Kong 2025 that for Bitcoin, market liquidity may have returned to the level before the FTX crash, but for other tokens, liquidity is still insufficient. The market is still dominated by the price of Bitcoin, and the liquidity of other tokens is affected. In addition, the launch of new tokens will extract liquidity from existing tokens. For example, when TRUMP was launched, a large amount of funds flowed out of other tokens, causing the market to fall sharply. This phenomenon occurs every time a new token is launched.
Evgeny Gaevoy also said that the market has not fully priced in the improvement of the regulatory environment, and despite many positive regulatory developments, the market is still in a wait-and-see state.
Evgeny Gaevoy finally said that he believes that the prices of Bitcoin and Ethereum will continue to rise, and although there may not be a full bull market, there will be some fluctuations in the market. Investors will pay more attention to those protocols that really have practical uses and sustainable economic models.

data.title: Metaplanet conducts 10-for-1 stock split to increase market access, total shares issued rises to 391.6 million
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News: Japanese listed company Metaplanet announced a 10-to-1 stock split, which will take effect on April 1, 2025, to reduce per-share transaction costs and improve market liquidity. The decision was made after a sharp rise in Metaplanet's share price, which increased the minimum investment cost for retail investors.
The stock split will apply to shareholders who have completed registration as of March 31, and each existing share will be converted into ten new shares, which will increase the total number of issued shares from approximately 39.1 million shares to 391.6 million shares. The company said the move is aimed at reducing investor unit prices to increase market participation.

data.title: LBank to Launch CORN (CORN) Pre-Market Trading
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News LBank announcement shows that LBank will launch CORN (CORN) pre-market trading at 20:00 (SGT) on February 19, and open the CORN/USDT trading pair. It is reported that the delivery of CORN pre-market trading will be carried out 30 minutes before the official opening, and LBank will redeem CORN pre-market trading and official trading tokens 1:1.
At the same time, in order to protect the interests of users to the greatest extent, LBank launched a price protection limited event to provide all-round protection for CORN spot pre-market trading. When the pre-market trading ends, if the price of CORN held by the user is lower than the average pre-market trading price of the industry market, the loss caused by the price drop will be subsidized by LBank, up to 50USDT, and the subsidy will be issued to the user's spot account in the form of USDT within 3 days after the end of the pre-market trading.

data.title: BitMart will launch PI perpetual contract
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News BitMart will launch the Pi (PI) perpetual contract at 16:00 (UTC+8) on February 20, 2025. The PI/USDT trading pair will be opened at that time, with a maximum leverage of 50 times.
Pi Network is a new cryptocurrency and developer platform that allows mobile users to mine Pi tokens without draining the device battery.

data.title: The chairman of the South Korean Financial Committee supports the formulation of stablecoin regulatory policies as soon as possible, but is concerned about the strong performance of the US dollar
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News South Korea's Financial Services Commission (FSC) Chairman Kim Byung-hwan said the country needs to act "quickly" to regulate stablecoins, but is concerned about the "strength" of the U.S. dollar. 
Kim Byung-hwan said: "I also think that stablecoin regulation needs to be formulated quickly, but we need to take into account that the strength of the U.S. dollar is currently affected by underlying macro factors, including the strong performance of the U.S. economy." 
These remarks seem to indicate that the FSC is still on track to introduce stablecoin regulations later this year. Lawmakers and financial regulators are currently developing the "second phase" of the Virtual Asset User Protection Act, a cryptocurrency-related legislation that will take effect in mid-2024. 
Cryptocurrency advocates hope that regulators will include provisions on stablecoin regulation in this new bill. Major South Korean companies hope to launch stablecoins pegged to the U.S. dollar in the near future, and many companies are worried about falling behind technology competitors that issue stablecoins in the United States and elsewhere. But regulators have been dragging their feet on the issue, perhaps because of continued political uncertainty in the presidency. 
Yoon Han-hong, a member of the People's Power Party and chairman of the National Assembly's Political Affairs Committee, said: "To issue stablecoins, Korean companies need to buy U.S. Treasuries. This means they need to provide dollars to the federal government. This means that the dollars are flowing back to the U.S. government and basically disappearing. But our financial regulators are too dismissive of this concern." (Money Today)

data.title: Bitcoin's Bull Market Cycle Expected to Continue in 2025
data.description:
According to PANews, CryptoQuant CEO Ki Young Ju expressed his belief that Bitcoin will not enter a bear market this year and remains in a bull market cycle. He anticipates that Bitcoin's price will eventually rise, although it may experience significant fluctuations. Ju suggested that even if the price drops 30% from a historical high, such as $110,000, to $77,000, the bull market cycle could persist, aligning with past market trends. Additionally, he noted that Strategy's (formerly MicroStrategy) Bitcoin cost benchmark is $65,033.

Earlier today, it was reported that the cost basis for Bitcoin in ETFs or custodial wallets is $89,000.

data.title: Cyprus police warn of surge in SMS scams targeting cryptocurrency users
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News Cyprus Police Cyber ​​Crime Unit warned on Tuesday that the number of text message (SMS) scams targeting cryptocurrency investors has surged. The notice showed that scammers impersonated Binance employees to trick users into revealing their login credentials. Once these details are obtained, scammers can access accounts and steal funds.
These SMS scam messages usually come from seemingly legitimate sources, claiming that unauthorized login attempts from foreign countries have been detected. The recipient is then asked to call a phone number to resolve the problem, which may eventually reveal login credentials. (Cyprus Mail)

data.title: CMC Altcoin Season Index Stands at 40, Marking a 2 Point Shift
data.description: According to CoinMarketCap (CMC) data, the CMC Altcoin Season Index currently stands at 40, reflecting a increase of 2 points compared to the previous day. The index measures market performance by analyzing the top 100 cryptocurrencies by market capitalization. Over the past 90 days, 40 of these altcoins have outperformed Bitcoin in terms of price growth. The CMC Altcoin Season Index is used to determine whether the current cryptocurrency market is in an altcoin-dominated season. It is based on the performance of the top 100 altcoins relative to Bitcoin over the past 90 days.
